evidence: R2 running-config: interface Serial0/1/0 has "ip access-group R2_IN in" (get_running_config for R2).
R2 access-list R2_IN: sequence 10 deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.5; sequence 20 deny ip any dst-host 224.0.0.6 (get_running_config for R2 -> access_lists).
R2 OSPF: Serial0/1/0 -> "neighbors": [] (get_ospf for R2 shows no neighbors on Serial0/1/0).
R1 OSPF: Serial0/1/0 -> neighbor_id 2.2.2.2 addr 10.0.0.2 state ospf-nbr-full (get_ospf for R1) — asymmetry consistent with R2 dropping incoming OSPF packets.

plan_history.intent_description: OSPF adjacency on R2 Serial0/1/0 is down because inbound ACL R2_IN is denying OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6); restore adjacency by allowing OSPF hellos to reach R2.
plan_history.problem: Inbound access-list R2_IN applied to interface Serial0/1/0 on router2 is denying OSPF multicast addresses 224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6, preventing R2 from receiving OSPF hellos and forming adjacency with R1.
plan_history.fix_summary: Update access-list R2_IN to allow OSPF multicast (224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6) on router2 Serial0/1/0 while retaining other ACL intent.

parameters: remove sequence 10 deny ip any host 224.0.0.5
remove sequence 20 deny ip any host 224.0.0.6
insert sequence 10 permit ip any host 224.0.0.5
insert sequence 20 permit ip any host 224.0.0.6

remaining_issues: Access-list R2_IN still contains deny entries for 224.0.0.5 and 224.0.0.6. Although it is not currently applied inbound to Serial0/1/0 (the immediate problem), consider removing or updating these deny entries to avoid future regressions if the ACL is re-applied.
